title: Indirect ELISA for detection of anti-HIV antibodies in cats immunized with a HIV gp120 peptide.
steps: The ELISA describes detection of antibodies against HIV proteins. It is an ELISA with a modification. The plate is coated with HIV peptides, fragment 254-274 of gp120.
The modification is that triplicates of 1/16 dilutions of cat sera in PBS non-fat milk are added to the micro-wells.
After incubation for 90 min at RT the microplate is washed 4X using PBS-Tween 20.
Then, 25 µl of recombinant protein LA-HRP diluted 1:1000 (Sigma-Aldrich) is added to the assay.
The microplate is incubated for 1h at RT and then, it is washed four times.
Tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) solution (50 μl) is added to each well. .
After a further incubation of 15 min in the dark, the reaction is stopped.
The microplate is read at 450 nm in a microplate reader at 450 nm. Geometric mean antibody titer is calculated using Perkins method.
creator: Angel A Justiz-Vaillant
